edges.evidence_quote: The Morabitos also maintain strong ties with the Maisano, Pansera, Versace, Zappia, Mollica, Criaco, and Scriva 'ndrine.

edges.evidence_quote: the Fukui-gumi, a Yamaguchi affiliate based in Osaka

Wah Kee.description: Wah Kee is a secret society based in Malaysia and Singapore since the nineteenth century. Founded in Singapore by Sū Guǎnghuá, it operates in multiple countries including Australia, Thailand, Vietnam, China, Hong Kong, the United States, Canada, and New Zealand. The group is involved in heroin trafficking in the region.

descriptions: Wah Kee has 08 as a rival according to the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: 24, 36, 08

descriptions: Wah Kee has 24 as a rival according to the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: 24, 36, 08

Lo Russo clan.description: The Lo Russo clan was a Neapolitan Camorra clan operating in the Miano area of Naples, formed in the late 1970s by the Lo Russo brothers. It was a member of the Nuova Famiglia and later the Secondigliano Alliance, involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and other crimes. The clan was considered overthrown after the arrests of its leaders and affiliates.

descriptions: Lo Russo clan listed as ally in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Lo Russo clan (defunct)

descriptions: The Lo Russo clan is a subgroup of the Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: The Camorra's organizational structure is divided into individual groups called 'clans'.

descriptions: Lo Russo clan is a Camorra clan.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2017, assets worth €20 million belonging to the Potenza brothers, who are considered to be linked to the Lo Russo clan , were seized in Lugano, including bank accounts in BSI .

descriptions: Birra-Iacomino allied with Lo Russo to carry out a murder.
edges.evidence_quote: Il compito fu affidato al clan alleato dei "Capitoni", ovvero i Lo Russo, in particolare alla squadra del reggente Raffaele Perfetto.

descriptions: Lo Russo clan is listed as a rival of Licciardi clan in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Lo Russo clan (defunct)

descriptions: A split from Misso clan was led by Salvatore Torino, who was linked to the Lo Russo clan.
edges.evidence_quote: un uomo legato al clan Lo Russo : Salvatore Torino detto ' 'o gassusaro '

descriptions: The Stabile and Lo Russo clans were in conflict in Chiaiano after previously being allies.
edges.evidence_quote: faida di Chiaiano : conflitto svoltosi nel corso del 2003 e 2004 a Chiaiano tra il clan Stabile e il clan Lo Russo, in precedenza alleati sotto la bandiera dell' Alleanza di Secondigliano.

descriptions: Lo Russo clan acted as intermediary between Di Lauro clan and secessionists.
edges.evidence_quote: During the Scampia feud, the Lo Russo clan had a role as an intermediary between the Di Lauro clan and the so-called 'secessionists'

descriptions: Lo Russo clan acted as intermediary between Di Lauro clan and secessionists.
edges.evidence_quote: During the Scampia feud, the Lo Russo clan had a role as an intermediary between the Di Lauro clan and the so-called 'secessionists'

descriptions: Licciardi clan is listed as a rival of Lo Russo clan in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Licciardi clan, Esposito-Genidoni clan

descriptions: Lo Russo clan was in conflict with the Stabile clan.
edges.evidence_quote: The Lo Russo clan, for its good organization, also had a leading role in a conflict with the Stabile

descriptions: Esposito-Genidoni clan is listed as a rival of Lo Russo clan in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Licciardi clan, Esposito-Genidoni clan

descriptions: Lo Russo clan merged into the Nuova Famiglia federation.
edges.evidence_quote: After its formation, it immediately merged into the Nuova Famiglia (NF), a federation of Anti-Cutolo Camorra clans

descriptions: Piccirillo clan is listed as an ally of Lo Russo clan in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Scissionisti di Secondigliano, Vastarella clan, Piccirillo clan, and formerly Secondigliano Alliance

descriptions: Lo Russo clan joined the Secondigliano Alliance after the NF dissolved.
edges.evidence_quote: After the end of the NF alliance, the Lo Russo clan joined the newly formed Secondigliano Alliance

descriptions: Lo Russo was previously allied with the Alleanza di Secondigliano.
edges.evidence_quote: in precedenza alleati sotto la bandiera dell' Alleanza di Secondigliano

descriptions: Vastarella clan is listed as an ally of Lo Russo clan in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Scissionisti di Secondigliano, Vastarella clan, Piccirillo clan, and formerly Secondigliano Alliance

descriptions: Lo Russo clan was initially an ally but later split and engaged in a violent gang war with the Secondigliano Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: The Lo Russo clan , then an important ally of the Alliance, disagreed and repackaged the shipment for sale on the streets, resulting in the deaths of many. ... The Lo Russo clan eventually split from the alliance, leading to a violent gang war against the other clans which claimed 120 lives in Naples and the surrounding region.

descriptions: Lo Russo clan is a member of the Secondigliano Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Apart from the Licciardi clan, the alliance included the Contini, Mallardo, Lo Russo, Stabile, Prestieri, Bocchetti and Di Lauro clans.

descriptions: The Torino faction was supported by the Lo Russo clan.
edges.evidence_quote: appoggiati dai Lo Russo di Miano

Boricua Popular Army.description: The Boricua Popular Army (EPB), also known as Los Macheteros, is a clandestine militant and insurgent organization based in Puerto Rico that campaigns for Puerto Rican independence from the United States. Founded in the 1970s, it was led by Filiberto Ojeda Ríos until his death in 2005. The group has claimed responsibility for bombings, attacks on US military personnel, and a 1983 Wells Fargo robbery. After 2005, it transitioned into an activist group.

descriptions: EPB worked in conjunction with FALN in 1979.
edges.evidence_quote: In September 1979, the EPB revealed that it was working in conjunction with the Volunteers, FARP and FALN.

descriptions: EPB worked in conjunction with FARP in 1979.
edges.evidence_quote: In September 1979, the EPB revealed that it was working in conjunction with the Volunteers, FARP and FALN.

descriptions: EPB joined the FARP and Volunteers in planting bombs in 1986.
edges.evidence_quote: On October 28, 1986, they joined the FARP and the Volunteers in planting eight bombs in militarily sites across Puerto Rico.

descriptions: Infobox lists Boricua Popular Army as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Boricua Popular Army

descriptions: Ñetas identify with the ideology of Ejército Popular Boricua.
edges.evidence_quote: La pandilla Ñetas se identifica con el Ejército Popular Boricua y su filosofía revolucionaria en la búsqueda de la independencia de Puerto Rico.

descriptions: The two sets were in a conflict that led to a mass shooting in Sacramento in 2022.
edges.evidence_quote: the shooting was related to a conflict between the local Garden Blocc Crips and Del Paso Heights Bloods gangs

descriptions: Infobox lists Garden Blocc Crips as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Garden Blocc Crips Sacramento Ca

descriptions: Lo Presti formed alliances with the Stalteri 'ndrina in Toronto.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto rapporti di affari con la Famiglia Inzerillo -Spatola-Gambino di Palermo , attraverso la quale strinse alleanze anche oltreoceano con la Famiglia Gambino di New York e con le famiglie della 'Ndrangheta in Canada degli Zito , dei Commisso , dei Racco, degli Stalteri a Toronto e dei Cotroni a Montréal .

descriptions: Cozzolino clan is a rival of Vollaro clan per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mazzarella clan, Birra clan, Cozzolino clan

descriptions: Schultz sought permission from the Commission, indicating a subordinate relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Schultz asked the Commission, the governing body of the American Mafia, for permission to kill Dewey

descriptions: Murder, Inc. carried out the Commission's order to kill Dutch Schultz.
edges.evidence_quote: the Commission ordered Louis Buchalter to eliminate 'The Dutchman'

descriptions: Goda-ikka's founder was a member of Kagotora-gumi, indicating a predecessor relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: The group was formed in 1948 as the Goda-gumi ( 合田組 , Gōda-gumi ) in Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi by Koichi Goda, then a member of an old yakuza clan, the Kagotora-gumi.

descriptions: The ABT fought wars with the Texas Mafia in 1995/1996, resulting in the TM being 'parked'.
edges.evidence_quote: Texas Mafia (TM) was 'parked' system wide, meaning they were no longer allowed to recruit members, after the ABT–TM wars in 1995/1996.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Dirty White Boys, Gulf Cartel, Texas Mafia

edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Cártel del Golfo, Dirty White Boys, Mafia de Texas

The Extraditables.description: Narcoterrorist organization created by Colombian drug lords in the early to mid 1980s to oppose extradition to the United States. Its members were largely drawn from the Medellín Cartel, led by Pablo Escobar. The group engaged in bombings, assassinations, and kidnappings to pressure the Colombian government. It was active from 1984 until Escobar's death in 1993.

descriptions: Cali Cartel listed The Extraditables as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The Extraditables (defunct)

descriptions: The Extraditables is listed as an ally of Medellín Cartel in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Muerte a Secuestradores , Guadalajara Cartel (defunct) , The Extraditables (defunct) , La Corporación (defunct) , Oficina de Envigado , Los Priscos (defunct) , Gallón Gang (defunct) , Galician clans , Gulf Cartel , Nicaragua (alleged) , Cuba (alleged) , Panama (alleged, under Manuel Noriega )

descriptions: The Extraditables were allied with the Medellín Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Medellín Cartel and associates

Piserà 'ndrina.description: The Piserà 'ndrina is a clan of the 'Ndrangheta, a criminal organization based in Tropea, Calabria, Italy. Active since the 1980s, it is allied with the Mancuso 'ndrina and has been in a feud with the La Rosa 'ndrina since the 1990s. The clan has been involved in violence, including the killing of municipal councilor Pasquale Piserà in 1982.

descriptions: La Rosa is in a feud with the rival Piserà clan.
edges.evidence_quote: sono in faida con il clan rivale dei Piserà

descriptions: La Rosa is in a feud with the Piserà 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: The La Rosa are in feud with the rival clan the Piserà 'ndrina

descriptions: Piserà 'ndrina is a sub-group of the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: The Piserà 'ndrina are a clan of the 'Ndrangheta

descriptions: The Piserà clan was in a feud with the La Rosa clan in the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni '90 del secolo scorso erano in faida con i La Rosa , anch'essi di Tropea .

descriptions: Piserà 'ndrina and La Rosa 'ndrina are in a feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Since the 1990s, they are in a feud with the La Rosa 'ndrina

descriptions: The Piserà clan is close to the Mancuso clan, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: I Piserà sono una 'ndrina di Tropea vicina ai Mancuso .

descriptions: Piserà 'ndrina and Mancuso 'ndrina are allies.
edges.evidence_quote: they are allied with the Mancuso 'ndrina

Rebels Motorcycle Club (Canada).description: The Rebels Motorcycle Club was an outlaw motorcycle club based in Western Canada, founded in Red Deer, Alberta in 1968. It was one of the dominant motorcycle clubs in Alberta during the 1970s-1990s, with chapters in Edmonton, Calgary, Moose Jaw, Saskatoon, and Toronto. The club engaged in violent conflicts with rivals such as the Grim Reapers and the Canadian Airborne Regiment. It was active until 2004.

descriptions: Rebels attempted to patch over to Bandidos in 2001.
edges.evidence_quote: Joey 'Crazy Horse' Morin, Vice President acting on behalf of Pete Sorenson (president) for the Edmonton chapter of the Rebels Motorcycle Club, first contacted the Bandidos with the aim of 'patching over'

descriptions: Rebels and Grim Reapers were rivals with violent clashes in the 1970s.
edges.evidence_quote: Throughout the 1970s the Rebels had multiple violent exchanges with their main rivals in the province- the Grim Reapers Motorcycle Club.

descriptions: Rebels chapters patched over to Hells Angels in 1998.
edges.evidence_quote: In September 1998, the Saskatoon Rebels were patched over to the Hells Angels

descriptions: Kings Crew listed as an ally of Rebels in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Satan's Choice , Kings Crew Motorcycle Club , Lobos and, later, the Hells Angels

descriptions: Lobos listed as an ally of Rebels in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Satan's Choice , Kings Crew Motorcycle Club , Lobos and, later, the Hells Angels

descriptions: Rebels merged with Loners Motorcycle Club in the 1980s.
edges.evidence_quote: after merging with the Loners Motorcycle Club of Saskatchewan in the 1980s

descriptions: Satan's Choice listed as an ally of Rebels in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Satan's Choice , Kings Crew Motorcycle Club , Lobos and, later, the Hells Angels

descriptions: Warlocks Motorcycle Club (Florida) listed as a rival of Rebels in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Grim Reapers , Warlocks Motorcycle Club (Florida) , EPS , RCMP , Canadian Airborne Regiment

descriptions: Mammoliti and Nava are related.
edges.evidence_quote: I Mammoliti sono una 'ndrina potente di Oppido Mamertina e Castellace quali fanno capo i Rugolo , i Nava, i Cosoleto, i Luppino e i Romeo con loro imparentate.

descriptions: Jie Ji was created by an associate of Wah Kee's founder, indicating a cooperative origin.
edges.evidence_quote: Zhāng Xióngjié (张雄杰) created Jié Jì (杰记) in Vietnam

descriptions: The Young Turks and the Stanfa faction were in conflict during the Third Philadelphia Mafia War.
edges.evidence_quote: After the Young Turks killed Stanfa loyalist Felix Bocchino in January 1992, Ciancaglini survived a retaliatory shotgun attack outside his home in South Philadelphia on March 3, 1992.

descriptions: The Stanfa faction and Merlino faction were in a violent feud during the Third Philadelphia Mafia War.
edges.evidence_quote: Martines joined the Philadelphia family in 1990 and became a key member of John Stanfa's faction which feuded with the 'Young Turks' led by Joey Merlino during the Third Philadelphia Mafia War of the early 1990s.

Asano-gumi.description: The Asano-gumi is a yakuza group based in Okayama, Japan, formed in 1945 as the Oyama-ikka and restarted as the Asano-gumi in 1952. It has been involved in conflicts with the Yamaguchi-gumi and Kyodo-kai. Since 1996, it has been a member of the anti-Yamaguchi federation Gosha-kai. The group has approximately 40 members.

descriptions: Asano-gumi had conflicts with Kyodo-kai in the late 20th century.
edges.evidence_quote: The Asano-gumi caused conflicts with the Yamaguchi-gumi and the Kyodo-kai in the late 20th century.

descriptions: Asano-gumi and Yamaguchi-gumi were in conflict, with violent incidents including murders in 1987.
edges.evidence_quote: The Asano-gumi caused conflicts with the Yamaguchi-gumi and the Kyodo-kai in the late 20th century. In one notable case, Asano member(s) shot and murdered two Yamaguchi members in Kurashiki in 1987, as a retaliation for an earlier attack by Yamaguchi member(s) involving carving knives against two Asano members.

descriptions: Goda-ikka and Asano-gumi are both members of the Gosha-kai, cooperating against Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Since 1996, the Goda-ikka has been a member of an anti- Yamaguchi federation, the Gosha-kai, along with three other Chugoku -based organizations, the Kyosei-kai , the Kyodo-kai , the Asano-gumi , and the Shikoku -based Shinwa-kai .

descriptions: Kyodo-kai and Asano-gumi are both members of the Gosha-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: Since 1996, the Kyodo-kai has been a member of an anti-Yamaguchi federation named the Gosha-kai, along with three other Chugoku -based organizations, the Kyosei-kai , the Asano-gumi

descriptions: Kyosei-kai formed the Gosha-kai federation with Asano-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: has formed a new anti-Yamaguchi federation named the Gosha-kai since 1996 with three other Chugoku -based organizations, the Kyodo-kai , the Asano-gumi , the Goda-ikka , and the Shikoku -based Shinwa-kai .

descriptions: Forgione supported Alvaro in the faida di Sinopoli.
edges.evidence_quote: Gli Alvaro, appoggiati dalle famiglie Forgione e Violi, per vent'anni dovettero regolare i conti con i Filleti-De Angelis-Orfeo.

Kokusui-kai.description: Tokyo-based yakuza organization founded in 1958 by Masaji Morita. Originally a revivalist monarchist anticommunist group, it temporarily disbanded in 1965 after crackdowns. Controls Tokyo's Ginza district and was historically part of the Kantō Hatsukakai federation before becoming an affiliate of the Yamaguchi-gumi in 2005.

descriptions: Kokusui-kai and Sumiyoshi-kai were engaged in violent conflict over territory.
edges.evidence_quote: The suicide followed a rash of shootings and retaliations between Kokusui-kai and a rival gang, Sumiyoshi-kai, over gang territory, including the shooting death of Sumiyoshi-kai's boss.

descriptions: Kokusui-kai became an affiliate of Yamaguchi-gumi after the sakazuki ceremony.
edges.evidence_quote: As a result of the ritual, the Kokusui-kai became an affiliate of their former enemies, the Yamaguchi-gumi.

descriptions: Kokusui-kai became an affiliate of Yamaguchi-gumi in August 2005.
edges.evidence_quote: This changed in August 2005, when in a surprise move, the Kokusui-kai withdrew from the Kantō alliance and became an affiliate of the Yamaguchi-gumi.

descriptions: Kokusui-kai and Yamaguchi-gumi agreed to stop fighting, indicating a truce.
edges.evidence_quote: The shootings ended with an agreement between the Kokusui-kai and Yamaguchi-gumi to stop fighting on 8 February.

descriptions: Yamaguchi-gumi absorbed Kokusui-kai.
edges.evidence_quote: They absorbed the Tokyo-based gang Kokusui-kai

descriptions: The Errol Flynns are recognized as the precursors to these successor gangs.
edges.evidence_quote: Some of the most notable successors include the "Be Like Boys", "Dexter Boys" (an offshoot of YBI), "Schoolcraft Boys or SCB's", "SNS", "Fenkell Boys", "FMK" ("Fenkell Mafia Killers"), "7 Mile Killers or 7 Mile Dogs", "Linwood Boys", "Brewster Boys", "Jeffries Boys", and "8 Mile sconys", ("JBFL") "Joy Boys" or "Exit 9" All of these crews, excepting the "Be Like Boys" gang, are named after Detroit city streets or housing projects and some of these gangs still exist under new leadership.

descriptions: SAC and Silent Aryan Warriors jointly planned a bomb plot against Jewish athletes.
edges.evidence_quote: members of the group, along with members of the Silent Aryan Warriors (another white supremacist prison gang), allegedly planned to attack Jewish athletes at the 2002 Winter Olympics

descriptions: SAC and Silent Aryan Warriors were charged together in a drug and firearms distribution case.
edges.evidence_quote: 21 members of the SAC, Silent Aryan Warriors, and Noble Elect Thugs were charged in Utah with distribution of drugs ... and firearms

descriptions: Hudson Dusters battled the Fashion Plates for control of docks.
edges.evidence_quote: it constantly battled smaller rival gangs including the Fashion Plates, the Pearl Buttons, and the Marginals for control of the Hudson River docks throughout the 1900s

descriptions: Infobox lists Fashion Plates as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Fashion Plates

descriptions: Grande Aracri was allied with Russelli in the feud.
edges.evidence_quote: faida tra i Grande Aracri- Nicoscia -Capicchiano e Russelli e gli Arena - Trapasso - Dragone e Megna

descriptions: The Nicoscia are allied with the Russelli.
edges.evidence_quote: La 'ndrina Nicoscia è una cosca malavitosa o 'ndrina della 'ndrangheta calabrese che opera a Isola Capo Rizzuto , alleata con i Grande Aracri e i Russelli .

descriptions: Shamali Khilafat joined ISIS–K.
edges.evidence_quote: Notable among these are the Omar Ghazi Group, Shamali Khilafat, Gansu Hui Group, Harakat Khilafat Baluch, Jaysh ul Islam, Mullah Bakhtwar's group, the Shamsatoo group, and radical student groups in Pakistan and eastern Afghanistan.

Chijon family.description: South Korean gang active from 1993 to 1994, founded by Kim Gi-hwan and six other former convicts and unemployed workers. The gang targeted wealthy individuals and engaged in murder, extortion, kidnapping, cannibalism, and rape. It was dismantled after a series of arrests.

descriptions: Maggapa was a copycat gang inspired by the Chijon Family.
edges.evidence_quote: At the same time as the 1996 North Korean commandos infiltration incident , there was a South Korean nine-member copycat gang inspired by the Chijon Family. The copycat gang called themselves the Maggapa (막가파).

DeCavalcante crime family.description: The DeCavalcante crime family is an Italian-American Mafia crime family operating primarily in North Jersey, particularly in Elizabeth, Newark, Linden, and West New York. Founded around the 1900s by Phil Amari, it is considered by some to be the 'Sixth Family' due to its strong ties with New York's Five Families. The family engages in racketeering, gambling, drug trafficking, extortion, and other illicit activities. It maintains alliances with several other Mafia families, including the Gambino and Philadelphia families.

descriptions: The DeCavalcante family is a constituent family of the American Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: la familia criminal DeCavalcante de Nueva Jersey

descriptions: Bonanno family is allied with DeCavalcante crime family per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... DeCavalcante crime family

descriptions: Listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: DeCavalcante crime family

descriptions: The Cleveland family forged ties with the DeCavalcante crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Iocobacci inducted new members into the family, forged ties with the Chicago Outfit, the Detroit Partnership and the DeCavalcante crime family of New Jersey.

descriptions: Infobox lists DeCavalcante crime family as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: DeCavalcante crime family

descriptions: Members of the Colombo and DeCavalcante families attended a meeting together.
edges.evidence_quote: Induisi was placed at a meeting with the Colombo family, with Carmine Persico, Tom DiBella, Vic Orena, John Matera, Gennaro Langella, Allie Persico and Vic Regina, and from the DeCavalcante crime family, Giovanni Riggi, Sam DeCavalcante, Stefano Vitabile, and Jimmy Rotondo.

descriptions: DeCavalcante is a constituent family of the American Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: The family is part of the nationwide criminal network known as the American Mafia.

descriptions: Bonanno is listed as an ally of DeCavalcante in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno crime family

descriptions: Vincent Rotondo, a DeCavalcante capo, maintained close associations with the Bonanno family.
edges.evidence_quote: He maintained close associations with the Bonanno and Colombo families, including ties to Bonanno boss Phillip Rastelli

descriptions: Cleveland is listed as an ally of DeCavalcante in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Cleveland crime family

descriptions: Colombo is listed as an ally of DeCavalcante in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Colombo crime family

descriptions: Vincent Rotondo, a DeCavalcante capo, maintained close associations with the Colombo family.
edges.evidence_quote: He maintained close associations with the Bonanno and Colombo families

descriptions: Gambino family took control of DeCavalcante family after killing Vincent Rotondo, indicating a hostile takeover.
edges.evidence_quote: The murder was allegedly ordered by Gambino boss John Gotti, due to Rotondo's growing power becoming a threat to the Gambino family. After Rotondo's killing, the Gambino family took control of the DeCavalcante family, which had previously been affiliated with the Genovese family.

descriptions: DeCavalcante has especially strong relations with the Gambino family.
edges.evidence_quote: especially the Gambino family

descriptions: DeCavalcante maintains strong relations with the Gambino family.
edges.evidence_quote: The DeCavalcante family operates on the opposite side of the Hudson River from the Five Families of New York City, and maintains strong relations with each of the New York families, especially the Gambino family

descriptions: Genovese is listed as an ally of DeCavalcante in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Genovese crime family

descriptions: DeCavalcante family was previously affiliated with the Genovese family before being taken over by Gambino.
edges.evidence_quote: After Rotondo's killing, the Gambino family took control of the DeCavalcante family, which had previously been affiliated with the Genovese family.

descriptions: Lucchese is listed as an ally of DeCavalcante in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Lucchese crime family

descriptions: DeCavalcante maintains strong relations with the Patriarca crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: and the Patriarca crime family of New England

descriptions: DeCavalcante maintains strong relations with the Philadelphia crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: as well as with the Philadelphia crime family

descriptions: The Gambino family lists DeCavalcante as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Corleonesi, DeCavalcante crime family, Detroit Partnership, ...

descriptions: DeCavalcante family murdered Fred Weiss as a favor to Gambino boss John Gotti.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1989, he helped arrange the murder of publisher and sanitation business owner Fred Weiss, who was shot to death by the New Jersey DeCavalcante crime family as a favor to John J. Gotti.

descriptions: Genovese family lists DeCavalcante as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno crime family, Bufalino crime family, Buffalo crime family, Chicago Outfit, Cleveland crime family, Colombo crime family, Dallas crime family, DeCavalcante crime family, Detroit Partnership, Gambino crime family, Lucchese crime family, New Orleans crime family, Patriarca crime family, Philadelphia crime family, Pittsburgh crime family, Trafficante crime family, Hells Angels MC, Latin Kings, Pagan's MC, Purple Gang

descriptions: Genovese capo Nichilo indicted with DeCavalcante members for plotting murder of DeCavalcante acting boss.
edges.evidence_quote: In July 1992, Nichilo was indicted along with DeCavalcante crime family soldier Virgil Alessi, boss John Riggi and underboss John D'Amato for plotting to murder the DeCavalcante family's acting boss Gaetano "Corky" Vastola.

descriptions: Genovese family lists DeCavalcante as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno, Colombo, DeCavalcante, Detroit, Gambino, Lucchese (New Jersey faction), Philadelphia, K&A Gang

descriptions: CONVIVIR groups merged into the AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: Many of the CONVIVIR groups simply joined up with the Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC).

descriptions: Racial Volunteer Force split from Combat 18 but retains close links.
edges.evidence_quote: A group calling itself the Racial Volunteer Force split from C18 in 2002, although it has retained close links to its parent organisation.

descriptions: Outback Blacks, a sub-chapter of Black Power, ambushed Mongrel Mob Kawerau chapter in a gunfight.
edges.evidence_quote: On 13 January 2018, a gunfight erupted in Whakatāne, Bay of Plenty, when a funeral procession of the Mongrel Mob Kawerau chapter was ambushed by members of the Outback Blacks – a sub-chapter of Black Power.
